title: Test Agent finding
description: Schema for docs/generated/tests/_meta/findings/<slug>.yaml. A generation agent writes one of these when it observes suspect compiler behavior while authoring a test. The structured record lets a separate triage pass render an issue body and file it via `regenerate.py findings file`. Generation agents never file directly.

id.description: Stable slug. Must match the filename (without .yaml) so state lookups are unambiguous.

bundle.description: Bundle key in docs/generated/tests/_meta/manifest.yaml that surfaced the finding.

suspected_kind.description: Agent's classification at observation time. Triage may override before filing.

scope.description: Target / subsystem hint (spirv, hlsl, metal, wgsl, cuda, cpp, diagnostics, parser, ir, core-module). Drives the issue title prefix and conditional labels. Optional; omitted if not applicable.

title.description: Optional issue title. When absent, `regenerate.py findings file` derives one from scope + observed_summary. Operators may set or override before filing.

observed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the original observation.

command.description: Exact slangc / slang-test invocation that triggers the failure.

source_slang.description: Workspace-relative path to the .slang test file the agent was writing when it noticed the failure.

observed_summary.description: Single-line summary of what was observed (segfault, wrong codegen, missing diagnostic, ...).

exit_code.description: Process exit code, when relevant (139 for SIGSEGV under POSIX, etc.).

stderr_tail.description: Last lines of stderr (single line, may contain literal \n escapes). Optional; operator may add more during triage.

minimized_repro.description: Operator-supplied minimized version of the .slang source. When set, takes precedence over reading source_slang for the issue body. Empty by default.

claim.description: One-sentence statement of the correct behavior.

citation_kind.description: Strength of grounding. Triage treats kind=doc as weak; design docs are themselves LLM-generated and cannot be sole ground truth.

citation.description: Path / commit ref / spec section locating the cited source of truth.

agent_model.description: Model identifier of the generation agent (e.g. claude-opus-4-7).

source_commit.description: slangc git SHA at the time of observation.

doc_anchor.description: docs/generated/design/<file>.md#<section> the test was anchored to.

agent_self_assessment.description: Optional. Agent's own confidence and what it considered. Not used by triage gating; useful for operator review.
